column1: # LONE NIGHT — sound table (the space pivot, design.md §15.4)
# Owner: Jane. John: parse this, don't hardcode a path. See src/data/audio.ts.
# Tab-separated. Lines starting with # are comments. Blank lines ignored.
# id      the fixed event strings World/App already push (game/world.ts
#         playSfx calls, game/app.ts startMusic): hit, kill, hurt, death,
#         revive, win, boss_phase, pickup, gold, heal, evolve, chest,
#         levelup, music/theme. An id with no row here is silent — never a
#         bug. A row with no matching id is just inert.
#         curated) — never `space-assets/` (gitignored vendor drop). See the
# volume  0..1. Kept low on anything that can fire dozens of times a second
#         at 220-enemy density (hit/pickup) so it beds under the music
#         instead of turning into white noise; kept high on anything that
# loop    1 for music beds, blank for one-shots.
# Three music beds, not one — John built `AudioSink.setMusic(weights)` (a
# real crossfade, not a hard cut) driven off `World.musicIntensity`, which
# — literally "the same curve the horde already climbs," §15.4's original
# So all three ids can be *simultaneously active* at partial gain — pick
# tracks that can plausibly overlap during the crossfade window, not just
# ones that sound good alone. `boss_phase` stays a one-shot sting layered on
# top of the `boss` bed, not a replacement for it — one-shots never touch
